original_title: Octahedral–Tetrahedral Site Synergism in Spinel Oxides Enables 1700 h‐Stable Electrosynthesis of 2,5‐Furandicarboxylic Acid in Near‐Neutral Media
title: An FDCA electrocatalyst that survives 1700 hours without dying
blurb: Ru and Ni co-doped into a spinel cobalt oxide, with Ru sitting in octahedral sites and Ni in tetrahedral ones, split the labor for fully oxidizing HMF to FDCA in near-neutral media instead of the usual alkaline route that degrades the feedstock. The headline is 1700 hours of stable operation, the number these catalyst papers usually can't back up.

original_title: Image quality and lesion detectability in simulated [124I]MIBG cardiac imaging across 3-generation PET/CT scanners using a phantom model: Implications for activity and acquisition time reduction in clinical practice
title: Newer PET scanners might let you dose less MIBG for cardiac imaging
blurb: A phantom study runs simulated I-124-MIBG cardiac imaging across three PET/CT generations, old analog, digital, and long axial field-of-view, and finds the LAFOV scanners hold image quality and lesion detection while allowing less activity or shorter scans. It's phantom data, not patients, so read the reduction numbers as a ceiling, not a protocol.
venue: European Journal of Nuclear Medicine and Molecular Imaging

original_title: Implementation of a Multidisciplinary Radiopharmaceutical Dosimetry Program: Key Practical Considerations and Challenges.
title: What it actually takes to run a hospital dosimetry program for radiopharmaceutical therapy
blurb: University of Wisconsin's writeup on standing up a multidisciplinary dosimetry program for radiopharmaceutical therapy, coordinating radiation oncology and nuclear medicine physicians, medical physicists, and staff into something that produces patient-specific absorbed dose numbers. An operations playbook, not new data, but useful if your institution is still arguing over who owns dosimetry.

original_title: Thiols as Key HAT Catalysts in Photoredox Catalysis: Diversifying the Hydrofunctionalization Landscape of Unactivated Alkenes
title: Thiols quietly become the go-to HAT catalyst for stubborn alkenes
blurb: A survey of using thiols as hydrogen-atom-transfer catalysts in photoredox systems, aimed at unactivated alkenes that are usually too unreactive for radical chemistry. Thiols' kinetics and tunability let them pair with a range of photocatalytic modes for selective hydrofunctionalization. Reads like a map of a trend rather than one big new reaction.

original_title: Asymmetric Total Synthesis of (+)‐Ineleganolide
title: Thirteen steps to ineleganolide's absurd cage skeleton
blurb: A 13-step asymmetric total synthesis of the norditerpenoid (+)-ineleganolide, whose [6,7,5,5,5] pentacyclic cage is exactly as annoying to build as it sounds. An intramolecular Diels-Alder sets the whole ring system as a single diastereomer in one shot, then a late-stage tandem epoxidation/Meinwald rearrangement forges the strained seven-membered ring.

original_title: Combined therapy with ALK inhibitors and a novel EGFR-targeting antibody‒drug conjugate as a strategy for ALK-rearranged non–small cell lung cancers
title: Pairing an EGFR ADC with ALK inhibitors to cover NSCLC's escape routes
blurb: LR004-VC-MMAE, an EGFR-directed antibody-drug conjugate already showing activity in EGFR-positive xenografts, gets tested alongside ALK inhibitors specifically in ALK-rearranged NSCLC, a combination the paper says hasn't really been tried. The logic is resistance-proofing: hit two drivers instead of betting the tumor stays put on one.

original_title: Adapting Bower's intramolecular aziridination reaction allows for a metal-free synthesis of N–H aziridines
title: N-H aziridines without the metal catalyst
blurb: Bower's intramolecular aziridination redone with tosylhydroxylamine and TFA in trifluoroethanol instead of a transition metal. Stereospecific, scales, and the substrate scope and functional group tolerance read broad enough to actually trust.

original_title: Dearomative [2 + 2] photocycloaddition to difluoro bicyclo[2.1.1]hexane bioisosteres
title: Photochemistry turns fluorinated arenes into bicyclic bioisosteres
blurb: Visible light drives a dearomative [2+2] that converts heteroaromatic gem-difluoro alkenes straight into difluoro bicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes, under mild conditions with broad scope and high diastereoselectivity. Another entry in the ongoing project of swapping benzene rings for saturated cages.

original_title: Targeting Copper in Cancer: Chelators to Counteract Cuproplasia and Ionophores to Promote Cuproptosis
title: Copper cuts both ways in cancer, so does the drug strategy
blurb: Tumors need copper to grow (cuproplasia), but flood the cell with it and it dies instead (cuproptosis), so the review lines chelators up against the first and ionophores against the second. A few compounds have already reached clinical trials, but the pathways are still murky enough that nobody has a truly selective ligand for either side yet.

original_title: Sustainable Synthesis of Bio‐Based Furanic and Aromatic Amines Using an Optimized Whole‐Cell Transaminase–Decarboxylase Cascade in E. coli RARE
title: An E. coli cascade that stops fighting its own equilibrium
blurb: Pairing a transaminase with a pyruvate decarboxylase lets the decarboxylase drag off the pyruvate byproduct and pull transamination's usually unfavorable equilibrium forward, doubling conversion over the transaminase running alone. Works across a range of substituted benzaldehydes and throws off useful (R)-acyloins as a side product, not bad for a reaction that's normally thermodynamically stubborn.

original_title: Nickel-Catalyzed Cross-Electrophile Couplings of Tetraarylphosphonium Salts with Aryl Bromides
title: Turning phosphonium salts into biaryls without touching palladium
blurb: A nickel catalyst with magnesium turnings and LiCl activates the C-P bond in tetraarylphosphonium salts and couples them with aryl bromides to give biaryls in moderate to high yield, scaled up and tolerant enough for late-stage elaboration. The mechanism looks like it runs through an in-situ arylmagnesium, so the phosphonium salt is really just a masked aryl source.

original_title: Newman-Kwart Rearrangement Enabled by Flavin Photoredox Catalysis
title: Riboflavin does the Newman-Kwart rearrangement's dirty work
blurb: The NKR normally needs harsh conditions to turn phenols into thiophenols, but here cheap riboflavin under blue or green light gets it done at low catalyst loading across a broad substrate range. Mechanistic and spectroscopic work points to a photoinduced single electron transfer kicking things off, so a vitamin is now moonlighting as the photocatalyst.

original_title: Translation of a novel EphA2-targeted PET tracer from preclinical models to first-in-human studies in pancreatic cancer.
title: An EphA2 PET tracer goes straight to pancreatic cancer patients
blurb: Three Ga-labeled FZEAR tracers came out with over 99% radiochemical purity and held up on stability, and after xenograft work sorted EphA2-positive tumors from negative ones, the lead candidate Ga-FZEAR-2 went into a first-in-human pilot PET/CT in two PDAC patients. Two patients is a pilot, not a trial, but going bench-to-human in one paper is the part worth noticing.
venue: Eur J Nucl Med Mol Imaging

original_title: Precursor Engineering of Low-Coordinated Oxygen-Bearing Cu Electrocatalysts for Selective CO 2 Reduction toward Multicarbon Products
title: Giving copper electrocatalysts fewer neighbors to work CO2 with
blurb: Starting from a copper MOF precursor and running it through peroxide-assisted electrodeposition then in-situ reduction leaves the copper under-coordinated and studded with oxygen defects, and that low coordination is what pushes CO2 reduction toward C2+ products, hitting 80.5% selectivity at -1.3 V. The defects are doing the selectivity work here, not the copper itself.

original_title: Ultrasound-Assisted Polymorph Control in Reactive Crystallization of l -Glutamic Acid: Aiming for Consistently Producing Stable Form
title: Sonicating L-glutamic acid into staying in its stable form
blurb: Ultrasound gets applied during reactive crystallization of L-glutamic acid to steer the process toward the stable polymorph instead of whatever metastable form wants to show up. Polymorph control by brute acoustic force.

original_title: Iridium-Catalyzed Decarboxylative Annulation of 2- o -Carboranylbenzoic Acids with Internal Alkynes
title: Carboranylbenzoic acids lose CO2 and gain a naphtho ring
blurb: An iridium catalyst strips the carboxylic acid off 2-o-carboranylbenzoic acids and annulates them with internal alkynes via B-H activation, landing exclusively on the B(4) position to give naphtho-o-carborane derivatives in moderate to excellent yield. The kinetics say B-H cleavage isn't the slow step, and the payoff is a set of emitters whose charge-transfer character shifts with how strong a donor or acceptor sits on the aryl group.

original_title: One-step cleavage and purification: a bifunctional IdeS-Protein A fusion protein for on-column generation of antibody fragments
title: One resin to cleave and purify your antibody fragments
blurb: A fusion of IdeS (cleaves IgG) and Protein A (binds Fc) glued into one protein, then immobilized on a column so you capture the antibody, cleave it in place, and wash away the leftover Fc and enzyme in a single step. Cuts a multi-step Fab/F(ab′)2 or VHH purification down to one pass, which matters most for the small-scale runs where the usual workflow is a slog.

original_title: Exclusive Markovnikov Synthesis of Tertiary Phosphine Sulfides from Secondary Phosphine Oxides, S 8 , and Alkenes
title: Tertiary phosphine sulfides, built Markovnikov-only
blurb: Secondary phosphine oxides plus elemental sulfur plus alkenes gives tertiary phosphine sulfides with exclusively Markovnikov selectivity. That's the whole claim on offer, but clean regiocontrol on a three-component coupling like this is worth a look.

original_title: Visible-Light-Induced Hydrosilylation Enabled by an In Situ Assembled Photoactive Complex
title: A hydrosilylation that grows its own photocatalyst
blurb: Mix the substrate, tris(trimethylsilyl)silane, and Cs2CO3 and the pile itself starts absorbing 456 nm light, kicking out silicon radicals with no exogenous photocatalyst or initiator added. Those radicals handle hydrosilylation of alkenes and alkynes and moonlight as halogen-atom-transfer reagents for dehalogenation, so one less bottle on the shelf.

original_title: Study of Carfilzomib, Lenalidomide, Dexamethasone and Belantamab Mafodotin in Multiple Myeloma
title: Bolting belantamab mafodotin onto the standard myeloma cocktail
blurb: A trial adding belantamab mafodotin to carfilzomib, lenalidomide, and dexamethasone in multiple myeloma, tracking response along with the good and bad effects of the combination. Registry-style description, so it's the design on record, not results.

original_title: Synthesis of thieno[2,3- b ]indoles by Cloke–Wilson rearrangement of spiro donor–acceptor cyclopropanes
title: Spiro cyclopropanes into sulfur-fused indoles, air does the last step
blurb: Lawesson's reagent swaps a carbonyl for a thiocarbonyl on spiro donor-acceptor cyclopropyl oxindoles, which sets off a Cloke-Wilson rearrangement, and then plain air finishes with a barrier-less aromatization that DFT says is genuinely stepwise, not a shortcut in the mechanism drawing. Mild, tolerates a range of aryl substrates, and scales, which gets you thieno[2,3-b]indoles without needing a dedicated oxidant.

original_title: Unlocking ketene–Heck coupling via activation energy modulation in Pd catalysis
title: Getting ketenes into a Heck coupling
blurb: Dial in the activation energy of the Pd catalytic cycle and ketenes join a Heck coupling. The mechanistic hook is the whole story here, no yields or scope reported yet.

original_title: Competency assessment of synthesized titanate nanowires for preconcentration of terbium from monazite liquors
title: Titanate nanowires for fishing terbium out of monazite liquor
blurb: Hydrothermally grown titanate nanowires pull Tb from aqueous solution, with the isotherm fitting a Langmuir monolayer at 36.65 mg/g and kinetics running pseudo-second-order. Sorption comes out spontaneous and endothermic, desorption and recycling both checked out, so this reads like an actual preconcentration step for terbium processing rather than a benchtop curiosity.

original_title: Imaging xCT with Immuno-PET in Therapy-Resistant Non–Small Cell Lung Cancer
title: A PET tracer for the resistance marker before you commit to the ADC
blurb: xCT overexpression is one of the ways NSCLC shrugs off therapy, and it's also an antibody-drug conjugate target, so the authors built a Zr-89 immuno-PET tracer off the xCT antibodies HM30 and HM34 (DFO-conjugated through lysine) to image xCT expression and follow the antibodies' pharmacokinetics noninvasively. Specificity got checked by western blot, immunocytochemistry, and internalization assays before any of it went near a scanner.

original_title: Chemoenzymatic Synthesis of Well‐Defined α(2,8)‐ and α(2,9)‐Linked Oligosialosides
title: Chain-terminating sialic acid polymerization on purpose
blurb: Bacterial sialyltransferases NmC-PST and NmB-PST normally just keep tacking on Neu5Ac, so the trick is capping the sugar with a blocking group, a benzoyl ester at C-9 or a thioethyl ether at C-5, that stops the enzyme cold and yields defined-length α(2,8) or α(2,9) oligosialosides instead of a polymer smear. Strip the cap afterward (mild hydrolysis or reductive desulfurization) and it's back to natural Neu5Ac, ready for another round of extension. MD simulations back up why the block actually blocks.

original_title: Palladium-Catalyzed Unsymmetrical bis-Allylation of Active Methylene Compounds Using Vinyl Sulfoxonium Ylides and Allyl Esters
title: Two different allyl groups on one methylene, no base required
blurb: Pd catalysis stitches vinyl sulfoxonium ylides and allyl esters onto active methylene compounds to build unsymmetrical 1,6-dienes, mild and base-free, moderate-to-good yields across a broad substrate range. The intermolecular version runs through a ylide-derived allylic intermediate, but switch to the intramolecular variant and the mechanism flips to a cyclopropane-mediated decarboxylative allylation, a genuinely different path to the same diene skeleton.

original_title: Atomically Defined Photoswitchable Hybrid Nanostructures Built from Functionalized Norbornadienes and Cobalt Oxide Nanoislands on Au(111)
title: A photoswitch that stays electronically deaf to the metal underneath
blurb: Norbornadiene photoswitches usually get their photoresponse scrambled once they're anchored to a metal surface. Here the switch sits on cobalt oxide nanoislands grown on Au(111), using the oxide as insulation so the molecule stops talking to the gold underneath. Building-block groundwork for light-responsive interfaces more than a finished device.

original_title: Advancing Biochemical Molecule Registration, Representation and Search for New Drug Modalities
title: HELM can't keep up with peptides, oligonucleotides, and ADCs, and AstraZeneca is saying so out loud
blurb: A look from inside AstraZeneca at why cheminformatics infrastructure built for small molecules keeps failing the new drug modalities. Current representations, HELM included, can't capture the structural complexity of peptides, modified oligonucleotides, and ADCs because they're stuck being either purely atomistic or purely sequence-based, and the gaps go beyond technical into how organizations manage the data.
venue: Journal of Chemical Information and Modeling

original_title: Spin-controlled enantioselective near-infrared photocatalysis with chiral MgO/Co3O4 nanoparticles
title: Chiral nanoparticles pick a tyrosine enantiomer under near-infrared light
blurb: D-/L-penicillamine-templated MgO-doped Co3O4 nanoparticles show real mirror-image circular dichroism and drive enantioselective hydroxylation of tyrosine to DOPA under NIR light. The D-particles consumed 92% of L-tyrosine against 54% of D-tyrosine, and doping in magnesium alone bumped activity by 23 points by opening up oxygen vacancies that help activate oxygen. Selectivity's real, just not remotely clean.

original_title: Calcination-driven structure–activity relationship of SnO₂–ZrO₂ mixed oxide catalysts for highly selective transfer hydrogenation of cinnamaldehyde to cinnamyl alcohol
title: Calcination temperature is the actual dial on this SnO2-ZrO2 catalyst
blurb: A cheap, co-precipitated SnO2-ZrO2 mixed oxide, fired anywhere from 400 to 800°C, transfers hydrogen from isopropanol to cinnamaldehyde and lands selectively on cinnamyl alcohol. The gains trace back to Lewis acidity, with Sn4+-Zr4+ pairing tuning crystallinity and surface area as the firing temperature climbs.

original_title: Photocatalytic, Scalable Anti-Markovnikov Hydrocarboxylation of Unactivated Alkenes via Amine-Mediated CO 2 •– Generation from Formate
title: Formate as your CO2 radical source, now working on the alkenes nobody could touch
blurb: Formate salts beat gaseous CO2 as a carboxylating reagent, but photochemical CO2 radical-anion generation from formate has mostly worked only on activated alkenes. This platform uses an amine-mediated route to push anti-Markovnikov hydrocarboxylation onto unactivated alkenes too, chain length not seeming to matter, via a tertiary aminium radical cation doing the hydrogen abstraction.

original_title: Palladium-Catalyzed Meta-Selective Suzuki Coupling of 3,4-Dichloropyridine through N -Oxidation/Deoxygenation
title: Suzuki coupling finds the meta position by detouring through an N-oxide
blurb: Palladium-catalyzed meta-selective Suzuki coupling on 3,4-dichloropyridine, routed through N-oxidation and deoxygenation to get regiochemistry that pyridines don't hand you for free.

original_title: Photocatalytic halogen atom transfer enables general dicarbofunctionalization of alkenes with organic halides and CO2
title: Halogen-atom transfer opens alkene dicarbofunctionalization to the halides SET couldn't touch
blurb: Standard photoredox dicarbofunctionalization of alkenes with CO2 leans on single-electron transfer, which leaves aryl chlorides, electron-rich aryl bromides, and alkyl chlorides sitting there unreacted thanks to their nasty reduction potentials. Pairing halogen-atom transfer with SET under visible light gets those substrates moving, building carboxylic acids through two new C-C bonds instead of the usual one.

original_title: π‐Extended Salphen Scaffolds Enable CO 2 Electroreduction and Singlet Oxygen Generation
title: Salphen ligands reduce CO2 and throw off singlet oxygen on the side
blurb: Extending the π-conjugation across a new family of salen-type ligands, with phenylene bridges and BF2 complexes, gets electrochemical CO2-to-CO reduction up to 56% Faradaic efficiency, a solid number for an organocatalyst. The mechanism runs through the ligand itself: single-electron reduction activates the framework so CO2 binds at the imine nitrogen. The same scaffold also generates singlet oxygen.

original_title: Animal Tumor Models for Boron Neutron Capture Therapy Studies (Excluding Central Nervous System Solid Tumors)
title: The animal models BNCT actually gets tested in, minus the CNS tumors
blurb: A rundown of the in vivo models used to push boron neutron capture therapy forward outside of CNS tumors, covering how different boron compounds, delivery routes, and dosing strategies get evaluated for stability, toxicity, and the host immune response to treated cells. More a map of what's been tried than new data.

original_title: Unlocking the activities of polyglutamylation enzymes in brain tubulin with top-down mass spectrometry
title: Top-down mass spec sorts out who's tacking glutamates onto brain tubulin
blurb: Tubulin's exposed C-terminal tails get built out with polyglutamate chains by TTLL enzymes, and running wild-type, TTLL1-KO, TTLL7-KO, and double-KO mouse brains through top-down MS confirms the division of labor directly: TTLL7 adds the first glutamate, TTLL1 extends the chain from there. A model that was mostly inferred now has the mass spec to back it.

original_title: Direct conversion of N2 and CH4 to valuable N-containing compounds via a plasma-activated catalytic process
title: N2 and methane, straight to nitrogen chemicals, no ammonia stop
blurb: A plasma-activated catalytic process converts N2 and CH4 directly into N-containing compounds, skipping the usual ammonia intermediate. Two dirt-cheap, famously unreactive feedstocks turned into something valuable in one step, assuming it scales past the demo reaction.

original_title: Chemo selective synthesis and antibacterial assessment of pyrazolo[3,4-d]pyrimidinones via a novel urea/thiourea-based ternary DES
title: One solvent plays reagent, catalyst, and solvent
blurb: A ternary deep eutectic solvent, ZnCl2:choline chloride:2thiourea, does triple duty making antibacterial pyrazolopyrimidinones, melting at a startlingly low 16°C. DFT and XPS back a ZnCl3-Ch+ complex hydrogen-bonding to the thiourea, and a urea-based version runs alongside it as the comparison case.

original_title: Strain‐Release Pentafluorosulfanylation of Carbonyl‐Containing Disubstituted Bicyclobutanes: A Fortuitous Path to SF 5 ‐Containing Oxa[2.1.1]bicyclohexanes
title: SF5 chemistry that turns up a bonus ring
blurb: SF5Cl adds across strained bicyclobutanes with real regio- and diastereocontrol, parking a bulky SF5 group next to a carbonyl on crowded tetrasubstituted cyclobutanes. Computational and structural work finds unusually tight SF5⋯C/N/O contacts, and the reaction also hands over an SF5-containing oxa[2.1.1]bicyclohexane nobody was chasing.

original_title: High-throughput mutational analysis of F(1)-ATPase by integrated cell-free protein synthesis and single-molecule rotation assay.
title: Cell-free synthesis turns ATPase mutagenesis into a numbers game
blurb: Pairing cell-free protein synthesis with a multiplexed single-molecule rotation assay lets them saturate-mutate F1-ATPase instead of testing variants one at a time. Scanning the catalytic base βE190 and the conserved βY307 near the phosphate-release tunnel, the βE190 results come out sharply binary: aspartate substitutions hold up, and little else does.

original_title: Interfacial Active Sites in Catalysts for Higher Alcohol Synthesis From CO 2 : A Review
title: Higher alcohols from CO2 aren't about one perfect site
blurb: A review of Cu, Co, and Mo catalysts for CO2-to-higher-alcohol conversion, arguing selectivity comes from cooperative interfacial ensembles rather than any single isolated active site. CO2 hydrogenation still has to out-compete methanation, RWGS, and methanol synthesis, so interfacial structure and promoter effects are what decide which way that competition goes.

original_title: Lithium Diisopropylamide Mediated Synthesis of 3-Amino-1,2-Disubstituted Indoles via Intramolecular Cyclization of Amides
title: LDA cyclizes its way to amino-indoles
blurb: Lithium diisopropylamide drives an intramolecular cyclization of amides to build 3-amino-1,2-disubstituted indoles. A base-mediated route to a substituted indole scaffold, nothing more elaborate claimed.

original_title: Blue Light Irradiated, Para Site-Selective C–H/C–H Cross-Dehydrogenative Coupling of Electron-Rich Arenes with Quinones
title: Blue light puts the quinone exactly at para
blurb: Eosin-Y and blue light, no metal catalyst, couple electron-rich arenes to quinones with clean para selectivity, TFA promoting and K2S2O8 oxidizing under open-flask conditions. It scales to grams, tolerates a broad range of functional groups, and some of the arylated quinone products are aimed at Alzheimer's-relevant chemistry.

original_title: Modular in vitro evaluation of buparlisib-polymeric nanomedicines in 2D and 3D models of glioblastoma
title: Two ways to chain buparlisib to a polymer, tested head to head
blurb: A modular pHPMA-buparlisib platform pits two conjugation chemistries against each other in glioblastoma: a disulfide linker that releases on intracellular glutathione, versus an azide handle for click chemistry. Buparlisib gets picked for its BBB permeability and well-mapped PI3K mechanism, and both go through 2D monolayer and 3D spheroid screening before anyone mentions animals.

original_title: Green supported of palladium nanoparticles over the CNT/Fe 3 O 4 nanoparticles: A recyclable catalyst for Buchwald–Hartwig C–N cross coupling reaction and study of its anti-breast cancer properties
title: A magnetic Pd catalyst that also gets screened against breast cancer
blurb: Pd nanoparticles (25–30 nm) parked on CNT/Fe3O4 pull double duty: a magnetically recoverable catalyst for Buchwald-Hartwig C-N coupling, turning aryl halides into aryl amines at 85-95% yield, and separately tested for anti-breast-cancer activity. Two unrelated selling points bolted onto one nanocomposite, which is either efficient or a sign the paper couldn't decide what it was about.

original_title: [ 124 I]I-cobimetinib PET enables noninvasive tracking of MEK inhibition in MAPK-driven cancers
title: A PET tracer to catch MEK inhibitors actually hitting their target
blurb: [124I]I-cobimetinib radioiodinates off a boron pinacol precursor at 64±8% radiochemical yield and >98% purity, then holds up well, over 90% intact in PBS and 81% in serum at 72 hours. It's also potently MEK-dependent cytotoxic in vitro. Still preclinical, but a noninvasive way to confirm MEK inhibition is actually happening in MAPK-driven tumors instead of inferring it from tumor shrinkage.

original_title: Synthesis of 1,3-Disubstituted 3-Azabicyclo[3.2.0]heptane Libraries for Fragment-Based Drug Discovery
title: Fragment libraries built on an azabicycloheptane scaffold
blurb: A set of 1,3-disubstituted 3-azabicyclo[3.2.0]heptanes made as a fragment library for drug discovery. No biology attached here, just the building blocks.

original_title: Iodide-Promoted Oxidative S-Arylation of Sulfenamides with Phenols for the Synthesis of Sulfilimines
title: Sulfilimines from sulfenamides and phenols, iodide doing the work
blurb: Oxidative S-arylation of sulfenamides with phenols, promoted by iodide rather than a metal catalyst, to build sulfilimines.

original_title: Four-Step Continuous-Flow Total Synthesis of 4-Chloro-6,7-dimethoxyquinoline, a Key Intermediate for Tyrosine Kinase Inhibitors
title: Cabozantinib and tivozanib's shared intermediate, now made in flow
blurb: 4-chloro-6,7-dimethoxyquinoline feeds into two approved kinase inhibitors, tivozanib and cabozantinib. This route builds it in four continuous-flow steps from 1,2-dimethoxybenzene (Friedel-Crafts acylation, nitration, hydrogenation, chlorination), chaining the reactions, quenches, and phase separations into one end-to-end line instead of running it in batch. Main payoff is defusing the nitration step, which they backed up with an actual kinetic model rather than just running it safer and hoping.

original_title: Highly Efficient Isolation and Rapid Detection of Pancreatic Cancer-Derived Small Extracellular Vesicles by Specific Aggregation from Aptamer-Functionalized DNA Long Chains
title: Fishing pancreatic cancer's exosomes out with DNA chains
blurb: Aptamer-functionalized DNA long chains selectively aggregate small extracellular vesicles shed by pancreatic cancer, giving a fast way to pull them out and detect them.

original_title: Boron Amidate as a Modular Anionic Catalyst for Sequential Electron-Donor/Hydrogen-Atom-Transfer Catalysis
title: One catalyst that hands off electron transfer to H-atom transfer
blurb: EDA complex excitation is a common way to generate radicals without a photocatalyst, but building a catalyst that also handles the hydrogen-atom-transfer step has lagged behind. This anionic boron amidate does both in one framework: the conjugated core runs the donor-acceptor assembly, and tweaking the nitrogen substituent tunes donor strength, acidity, and N-H bond strength independently. Radical generation from EDA excitation then feeds directly into productive HAT.

original_title: Optimization of a Resolution Process Allowing Access to Both Enantiomers of the Versatile Chiral Phosphine Ligand sSPhos on Scale
title: Now you can get either sSPhos enantiomer, on scale
blurb: sSPhos is one of the few genuinely useful chiral monodentate phosphines, earning its keep in atroposelective Suzuki-Miyaura coupling and dearomatization chemistry. The old resolution, a quinidine salt, only ever gave the R enantiomer, at small scale, capped near 98% ee, and needed a presynthesized dihydroquinine trifluoroacetate salt as a starting point. The reworked process reaches both enantiomers at scale and drops that extra salt-prep step.

original_title: Real-time electrical monitoring of enzymatic catalytic dynamics at the single-molecule level.
title: Watching a P450 enzyme think, one electron at a time
blurb: Single-molecule conductance measurements catch CYP1A1 metabolizing benzo[a]pyrene in real time: alpha-helix rearrangements and heme redox transitions both shift how well the protein conducts charge as it works. Conductance tracks benzo[a]pyrene concentration closely enough to extract a Michaelis constant (24.2 to 43.2 uM) and resolve several distinct conductance states through the catalytic cycle. Bulk assays average this kind of microsecond-scale fluctuation right out of existence.

original_title: Topological Control of Paratropicity via Alternative Ring Fusion in Indacenodithiophene Isomers
title: Tuning antiaromaticity by how you fuse the rings, not just their size
blurb: A series of isomeric indacenodithiophenes with different ethynyl substituents, including bulkier ones (TMS, adamantyl) that held up well enough to isolate. NICS values and ring-current plots show these iso-IDTs carry less antiaromatic character than indenofluorene and the previously reported syn- and anti-fused IDT isomers, meaning the ring-fusion pattern itself is a lever for dialing paratropicity.

original_title: The Mechanism of Visible‐Light Photochemical Rearrangements of Conjugated Amide Enolates
title: Amide enolates that rearrange when you shine light on them
blurb: Deprotonating conjugated amides gives colored lithium trienolates that absorb visible light, and hitting them at the right wavelength triggers skeletal rearrangements, bond migrations and ring expansions included. The mechanism runs through C-N bond homolysis at an S1/S0 crossing, producing a singlet biradical that recombines into the rearranged amide, with the molecular framework deciding the regiochemistry.

original_title: Recent advances in the organocatalytic enantioselective construction of alkynyl-containing tetrasubstituted carbon stereocenters
title: Fifteen years of building alkynyl quaternary stereocenters with organocatalysis
blurb: A review of organocatalytic asymmetric routes to alkynyl-containing tetrasubstituted carbon stereocenters, a motif that turns up constantly in bioactive molecules and is useful for further derivatization. Covers roughly 2010 to 2025: mechanisms, applications, and where the current strategies still come up short.

original_title: Radnostix Acquires Lucerno Dynamics Assets Expanding Radiopharma Medical Device Portfolio - TradingView
title: Radnostix picks up Lucerno Dynamics' device assets
blurb: Radnostix has acquired Lucerno Dynamics' assets, folding them into its radiopharma medical device portfolio. Another quiet consolidation move in a space that's had a lot of them lately.

original_title: Rapid Access to Photoswitchable RNA Binders: Fluorination Enhances Protein Rescue by Exon Inclusion
title: A light switch for RNA binders, and fluorine makes it work better
blurb: RNA is a miserable drug target because the functional motifs are transient and context-dependent, so binders are hard to design and even harder to turn on and off. This platform makes photoswitchable RNA ligands fast instead of through the usual long synthesis slog, and fluorinating them improves how well they rescue protein expression via exon inclusion. A tool paper, but a genuinely quick route to switchable RNA chemistry.

original_title: PSMAddition Trial of ADT, ARPI, and Lutetium-177 in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer - Fred Saad - UroToday
title: Lu-177-PSMA moves earlier, into hormone-sensitive prostate cancer
blurb: PSMAddition pairs Lutetium-177-PSMA with ADT and an ARPI in metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer, not the castration-resistant setting where Lu-177-PSMA already has approval. Whether pushing it earlier in the treatment line adds benefit or just front-loads the same effect is the thing to watch.

original_title: Controlled ROS generation in pyridinium-functionalized nanocatalysts for selective photooxidation
title: Pyridinium nanocatalysts that pick their ROS on purpose
blurb: Ultrasonic fragmentation of a pyridinium-functionalized coordination polymer makes nanoparticles that photocatalyze alcohol oxidation, with water in the mix steering the chemistry toward singlet oxygen to boost selectivity. A cheap knob for tuning photooxidation, not a new reaction.

original_title: High-throughput small-angle X-ray scattering reveals effective structure factor transitions linked to high-concentration antibody viscosity
title: SAXS catches antibody viscosity trouble before you ever concentrate the sample
blurb: High-concentration mAb formulations get too viscous for subcutaneous dosing, and the usual way to find out is late and eats a lot of sample. This high-throughput synchrotron SAXS protocol picks up the protein-protein interactions that cause it at dilute concentrations instead: 21 mAbs screened, with an initial set of 10 run from 1 to 150 mg/mL to spot the structure factor transitions that flag risk. Discovery-stage triage, right when you'd actually want the warning.

original_title: Engineering BN‐Doped Open‐Shell Polycyclic Hydrocarbons for Enhanced Photothermal Conversion and Therapy
title: BN-doped open-shell hydrocarbons built to turn light into heat
blurb: Inorganic photothermal agents don't biodegrade well, and plain carbon ones convert light to heat inefficiently, so this is a new class of atomically precise BN-doped polycyclic hydrocarbons with stable diradical and tetraradical character instead. The boron/nitrogen doping tunes charge distribution across the conjugated skeleton to push photothermal conversion efficiency up for therapy. Exotic radical electronics aimed at a specific biomedical job.

original_title: Cyclopropylamine‐Derived Distonic Iminium Radicals: Photoinduced Strategies in Chemo‐ and Stereo‐Selective Synthesis
title: A review roundup of cyclopropylamine radical chemistry
blurb: Cyclopropylamines undergo single-electron oxidation and strain-release ring opening to form distonic iminium radicals, and this review rounds up what's been built from them: ring-opening functionalization, formal [3+2] cycloadditions, radical cascades, asymmetric bond formation. Handy as a state-of-the-art summary, but it's a review, not new results.

original_title: Proteoform-specific enrichment of phosphopeptide isomers by polymer-based synthetic receptors
title: Sorting phosphopeptide isomers by proteoform, not just mass
blurb: Polymer-based synthetic receptors doing proteoform-specific enrichment of phosphopeptide isomers, separating out isomers that mass spec alone tends to blur together.

original_title: Electronic effects on the stability of heteroleptic nickel(II) complexes with aromatic and aliphatic ligands
title: What actually makes a heteroleptic nickel complex stable
blurb: Six Ni(II) complexes pairing a xanthate ligand (n-butyl or 2-methoxyethyl) with a nitrogen donor, ethylenediamine, bipyridine, or phenanthroline, to see how the sulfur/nitrogen coordination environment affects thermochemical stability and excited-state behavior. Ligand-design bookkeeping, but the kind that tells you which combinations are worth building on.

original_title: Soft Segment Engineering of Bio-Based Poly(propylene 2,5-furandicarboxylate) for Enhanced Toughness and Spinnability
title: Making bio-based furan polyester tough enough to spin into fiber
blurb: PPF is a biobased alternative to petroleum polyesters, but it's brittle, crystallizes slowly, and has a narrow processing window for fiber spinning. Blending in PTMEG soft segments builds a tunable copolyester that picks up toughness and spinnability without giving up PPF's thermal robustness.
venue: Journal of Polymers and the Environment

original_title: Ligand directed nanocarriers enable brain selective neuroprotection through optimized blood brain barrier transport and translational design
title: A review on getting nanocarriers past the blood-brain barrier without just sticking to it
blurb: A mini-review arguing that ligand-targeted nanocarriers only help if receptor engagement actually drives transcytosis, not merely binding to the endothelium. It walks through what has to line up for that: receptor accessibility, affinity/avidity balance, ligand density, PEG shielding, cargo properties, disease state. No new data, just a framework for reading past the usual ligand catalogs.

original_title: Injectable hydrogel scaffolds with sustained bioactive release and multilevel architecture for cartilage repair.
title: Cartilage hydrogel that gels in 30 seconds and doesn't dump its payload all at once
blurb: Chitosan and gelatin crosslinked via Schiff base, reinforced with strontium, and loaded with coaxial electrospun fibers that bridge the pores into a layered ECM-like scaffold. Shear-thins for injection, gels in under 30 seconds, and the fiber encapsulation tamps down the initial burst so the strontium trickles out over time instead of dumping.

original_title: A Practical and Scalable-Oriented Enantioselective Synthesis of ( S )-Autumnaline
title: An 11-step route to (S)-autumnaline that skips the cyanide and the column
blurb: A process-friendly enantioselective synthesis of (S)-autumnaline, the biosynthetic precursor to the colchicine alkaloids, built on a Bischler-Napieralski cyclization and asymmetric imine reduction. The practical wins are a cyanide-free Darzens homologation and purification by tartrate salt formation instead of chromatography. Eleven steps, 13% overall yield, configuration nailed down by CD plus computation.

original_title: Assessing collagenase-a and type-II mediated degradation of human type-I collagen by photoacoustic spectroscopy toward clinical translation in cancer progression
title: Picking up collagen breakdown by sound instead of a stain
blurb: A compact photoacoustic sensor gives label-free spectral readouts as human type-I collagen gets chewed up by collagenase-A and collagenase type-II, with signal strength climbing as degradation progresses from intact to extensive. Pitched toward tumor matrix remodeling, but so far it's characterizing enzyme digestion in a dish, not a tumor.

original_title: Development and Preclinical Evaluation of (68)Ga-Labeled B7-H3-Specific Bicyclic Peptides as Immuno-PET Tracers for Oncology.
title: A Ga-68 tracer that actually tracks B7-H3 levels in tumors
blurb: Three Ga-68 bicyclic peptide tracers against B7-H3, differing only in PEG linker length, all came out with high radiochemical purity, decent stability, and fast blood clearance. The best binder, FZ1, hit 83 nM affinity, and its tumor uptake tracked B7-H3 expression across models: in H1299 tumors, overexpression tripled uptake from 1.09 to 3.50 %ID/g. Preclinical only, but a clean specificity result.

original_title: Germanium ligand redox cooperativity: A key to ambiphilicity and switchable two- and four-electron transfer
title: A germanium ligand that swings between two- and four-electron redox
blurb: Redox cooperativity between the metal and a germanium ligand gives the complex ambiphilic character and lets it switch between two- and four-electron transfer.

original_title: A Ce 4+ Aluminum Hydride Complex
title: Cerium(IV) finally gets an aluminum hydride ligand
blurb: High-oxidation-state cerium and reducing hydride ligands are usually a redox mismatch that doesn't work, but this is the first Ce4+ aluminum hydride complex, made using tricks borrowed from Ce4+ alkyl synthesis. Structure confirmed by XRD, NMR, and UV-vis-NIR plus DFT, with the reactivity profile just starting to get probed by cyclic voltammetry and small-molecule reactions.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Oscillating Potential Engineering to Overcome Scaling Relationship Constraints in Alcohol Selective Oxidation
title: Oscillating the electrode potential to dodge the usual scaling-relationship wall
blurb: Alcohol-selective oxidation electrocatalysis engineered around the scaling relationships that normally cap selectivity, by driving the potential with an oscillating rather than fixed waveform.

original_title: Constructing dense three-dimensional networks in collagen/chitosan films via chemical cross-linking and metal ion coordination for improved water resistance and strength.
title: Crosslinking and metal coordination team up to make collagen/chitosan film actually waterproof
blurb: Plain collagen/chitosan packaging film falls apart in water and isn't very strong, so this pairs epoxy crosslinking (KH560 ring-opening onto collagen's carboxyls, forming Si-O bonds) with metal ion coordination to build a denser 3D network. Aimed at sustainable food packaging that can survive actually touching food.

original_title: AdvanCell locks in lease for US headquarters, radiopharmaceutical production near Boston - Fierce Pharma
title: AdvanCell locks in a US production site near Boston
blurb: A lease for radiopharmaceutical production near Boston, real estate as a signal they mean to actually manufacture stateside, not just talk about it.

original_title: Cu(II)-Catalyzed Cascade Cyclization and Oxidative Annulation to Access Functionalized 1 H -Furo[3,2- b ]pyrazolo[4,3- e ]pyridines
title: Cu(II) stitches furan and pyrazole onto pyridine in one pot
blurb: A cascade cyclization and oxidative annulation builds the fused furo-pyrazolo-pyridine core directly, with copper doing both the cyclization and the oxidation. One-pot instead of the usual step-by-step assembly of that scaffold.

original_title: Exploring the photodynamical landscape of biomimetic lumichrome–ephedrine-class amine complexes across femtosecond to millisecond regimes
title: Swap ephedrine for norephedrine and the excited state lasts four times longer
blurb: Lumichrome paired with sympathomimetic amines stands in for the flavin-amine chemistry behind neurotransmitter metabolism. In dichloromethane, H-bonding to norephedrine triggers an ultrafast proton transfer (about 800 femtoseconds) that leaves behind an anion surviving 5.4 nanoseconds, while the ephedrine and pseudoephedrine complexes burn out in 4.3-4.8 ns. A phenethylamine control pins the effect on the amine group itself, not just the H-bond.

original_title: CO 2 hydrogenation to CH 3 OH promoted by strong Cu x O–MgO interactions and non-thermal plasma under mild conditions
title: Plasma and a Cu-MgO interface push CO2 to methanol without heat
blurb: The selectivity isn't coming from the plasma alone but from a strong CuxO-MgO interaction that channels the plasma-driven hydrogenation toward methanol under mild conditions rather than the usual thermal route.

original_title: Fluorinated Silicon Quantum Dots for Broad-Spectrum Fluorescence Detection of Pyrethroids via Structure–performance Enhancement
title: Fluorinated silicon quantum dots tuned to spot pyrethroids
blurb: Structure tweaks to the quantum dots sharpen their fluorescence response across a broad spectrum, aimed at detecting pyrethroid pesticides.

original_title: When Octahedra Learn to Be Prisms: Unlocking a Record Deep‑UV NLO Crystal
title: Bending an octahedron into a prism gets a record deep-UV crystal
blurb: Instead of the usual octahedral cation coordination, they push cation-centered polyhedra into a triangular prismatic geometry to better arrange the crystal's conjugated borate rings. The payoff, Na2CsCa2(B3O6)2F, hits 5.5x KDP's second-harmonic response with a UV cutoff at 180 nm, aimed at replacing the toxic, brittle KBBF crystals deep-UV lasers currently rely on.

original_title: Photocyclization of 3DPA2FBN Yielding Core-Edge Fused Donor-Acceptor Cyanoarene Photocatalysts
title: A photocatalyst's breakdown pathway turns into two new photocatalysts
blurb: Blue light hits 3DPA2FBN, a common donor-acceptor cyanoarene photocatalyst, and rather than just falling apart it loses HF and photocyclizes, fusing the electron-poor cyanoarene core to the electron-rich diphenylamine periphery. The mono- and bis-cyclized products form regioselectively, are stable enough to isolate, and hold up across the standard photocatalytic benchmarks, so a degradation study doubles as a route to new catalysts.

original_title: Tivdak from Genmab - antibody drug aims at recurrent cervical cancer - ad-hoc-news.de
title: Tivdak, Genmab's cervical cancer ADC, back in the headlines
blurb: An antibody-drug conjugate already approved for recurrent cervical cancer getting another round of financial press coverage.

original_title: Characterization of the biofilm landscape of Bacillus subtilis by spatial microproteomics
title: Mapping a B. subtilis biofilm protein by protein, in place
blurb: Mass spec imaging straight off thin biofilm cross-sections turned up more than 285 unique proteoforms with minimal sample prep, and pairing it with bulk top-down proteomics builds out a spatial library of what's sitting where. A methods paper, but a genuinely high-resolution one.

original_title: Unleashing the potential of S-layer proteins for engineered living materials
title: S-layer proteins as scaffolding for engineered living materials
blurb: A review of two strategies for repurposing bacterial S-layer proteins, the 2D paracrystalline lattices that coat many prokaryote surfaces, as secretion and display platforms for engineered living materials. No new data, just a survey of what each approach buys you and where it falls short.

original_title: Novartis launches Radioligand Therapy for advanced prostate cancer treatment in India - BioSpectrum India
title: Novartis brings radioligand therapy for prostate cancer to India
blurb: Novartis is launching its radioligand therapy for advanced prostate cancer in the Indian market, extending a treatment class that's so far mostly stayed confined to the US and Europe.

original_title: Theoretical optimization and validation of Iodine-123 and Iodine-124 production via tellurium and antimony targets
title: Modeling the best way to make I-123 and I-124 before you build the target
blurb: A theoretical optimization of tellurium and antimony target routes for I-123 and I-124 production, checked against validation data rather than left as pure modeling. Useful if you're the one who has to actually irradiate the target.

original_title: Radioligand therapy in combination with CAR T cells overcomes the heterogeneous immunosuppressive prostate tumor microenvironment.
title: Pluvicto opens the door, PSCA-CAR T cells walk through it
blurb: Lu-177 PSMA RLT already treats mCRPC but the responses don't hold. CAR T cells never got solid tumors to work because antigen heterogeneity and a hostile microenvironment keep beating them. This pairs the two, Lu-177 RLT plus cyclophosphamide then PSCA-CAR T, and tests it in mouse models with both uniform and patchy antigen expression to see if the radiation softens up the tumor enough for the T cells to finish it. Mouse data only, no patients yet.

original_title: Precise Sequence Control and Thermal Characterization of N -Substituted Maleimide Copolymers Containing Hydrazine Structure
title: Maleimide monomers built from hydrazine, for once
blurb: Three new N-amino maleimides, dimethylamino, diphenylamino, and morpholino versions, made from N,N-disubstituted hydrazines and maleic anhydride, then radical-copolymerized with styrene to get actual sequence control instead of the usual statistical scramble. The thermal characterization is the payoff if you're chasing heat-resistant polymers with a dial you can turn.

original_title: Preclinical Evaluation of [ 225 Ac]Ac–Sibrotuzumab Employing a PEG 4 –Macropa Site‐Specific Chelation Strategy for Targeted Ablation of Cancer‐Associated Fibroblasts in Desmoplastic Tumors
title: Aiming Actinium-225 at the tumor's scaffolding instead of the tumor
blurb: Sibrotuzumab goes after FAP on cancer-associated fibroblasts rather than the cancer cells themselves, loaded here with Ac-225 through a site-specific Macropa-PEG4 chelate built to hold onto an alpha-emitter through its whole decay chain. Preclinical, in mice, with the usual radiochemical purity and stability readouts, but targeting the stroma around desmoplastic tumors instead of the tumor cells is the actual bet.
venue: Journal of Labelled Compounds and Radiopharmaceuticals

original_title: Recent advances in Camptothecin-derived antibody-drug conjugates
title: Camptothecin keeps finding new ADCs to ride in
blurb: A review of recent camptothecin-derived antibody-drug conjugates, the topoisomerase I poison that's been quietly carrying a lot of the field's momentum. It's a survey, not new data, just a map of where the linkers and payload tweaks have landed.

original_title: An anti-PMEL antibody-drug conjugate with a G(q/1)(1) inhibitor payload in GNAQ/GNA11-mutant melanomas: a phase 1 trial.
title: A phase 1 ADC built for melanoma's own driver mutation
blurb: An anti-PMEL antibody-drug conjugate carrying a Gq/11 inhibitor payload, run in a phase 1 trial specifically in GNAQ/GNA11-mutant melanomas, the uveal melanoma subtype those G-protein mutations drive. Matching the payload mechanism to the tumor's actual oncogene is the interesting part. Phase 1 means dosing and safety, not proof it works.

original_title: Peptide Thioester and Triazole Derivatives Through On-Resin Dual-Modification of Peptide Thiosulfonates.
title: Two modifications, one resin, on peptide thiosulfonates
blurb: A method for putting both a thioester and a triazole on the same peptide by dual-modifying peptide thiosulfonates while they're still on resin. The win here is fewer steps off resin, not a new molecule.

original_title: Peptide–Drug Conjugates for Targeted Delivery in Hematological Malignancies: From Design Principles to Clinical Application
title: Peptide-drug conjugates take their shot at blood cancers
blurb: A review of peptide-drug conjugates for hematological malignancies, covering targeting peptide choice, linker engineering, and payload selection as a cheaper, simpler alternative to ADCs for the relapsed and refractory cases that keep resisting current therapies. No new clinical numbers, just the design playbook.

original_title: Toward Precision Bioconjugation: Chemical Strategies for Site-Selective Cysteine Conjugation.
title: Getting cysteine conjugation to stop being a coin flip
blurb: A review of chemical strategies for site-selective cysteine conjugation, the recurring bioconjugation headache of wanting one defined attachment point instead of whichever cysteine happens to react first.

original_title: Ultrasound-Mediated OFF-to-ON Switching of Bone Regeneration via a Bioorthogonal Cell-Capture Hydrogel, Enabling Spatiotemporal Control and Monitoring.
title: Bone regeneration you switch on with ultrasound
blurb: A bioorthogonal, cell-capturing hydrogel that sits inert until ultrasound flips it from off to on, giving spatial and temporal control over bone regeneration plus a way to monitor it happening. A materials platform demo more than a therapy yet.

original_title: ADC Conjugation Strategies: From Technological Evolution to a Practical Selection Framework
title: Picking an ADC conjugation strategy stopped being a footnote
blurb: A framework for choosing ADC conjugation chemistry, tracing the move from first-generation stochastic labeling to newer site-specific methods now that Enhertu's success made it obvious how much drug-to-antibody ratio, pharmacokinetics, and manufacturability ride on that one decision.

original_title: Site-Specific Introduction of Non-Canonical Amino Acids into natural and engineered Non-Ribosomal Peptides
title: Teaching non-ribosomal peptide synthetases new amino acids
blurb: Nature's already built over 300 unusual amino acids into non-ribosomal peptides; this work screens NRP synthetases to see if they'll also take non-canonical amino acids that don't occur naturally, pushing the machinery past what evolution handed it.

original_title: Peptide modification via a mild carbonylative Suzuki-Miyaura reaction gives late-stage access to diaryl-ketones.
title: Diaryl ketones on peptides, no CO tank required
blurb: A carbonylative Suzuki-Miyaura coupling that swaps in a CO surrogate, so late-stage diaryl-ketone installation on peptides runs in water, open to air, with no gas cylinders or special rigs.

original_title: Development and preclinical evaluation of an asialoglycoprotein receptor 1-targeted antibody-drug conjugate in hepatic inflammation.
title: An ADC retargeted from tumors to a sick liver
blurb: Preclinical work on an antibody-drug conjugate aimed at asialoglycoprotein receptor 1, the usual liver-homing handle, but pointed here at hepatic inflammation instead of cancer.
